children.heading: Evaluations, parental consent, and reevaluations
children.label: a Evaluations, parental consent, and reevaluations

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A State educational agency, other State agency, or local educational agency shall conduct a full and individual initial evaluation in accordance with this paragraph and subsection (b), before the initial provision of special education and related services to a child with a disability under this subchapter.

children.heading: Request for initial evaluation
children.label: B Request for initial evaluation
children.text: Consistent with subparagraph (D), either a parent of a child, or a State educational agency, other State agency, or local educational agency may initiate a request for an initial evaluation to determine if the child is a child with a disability.

children.label: I
children.text: to determine whether a child is a child with a disability (as defined in 1401 of this title) within 60 days of receiving parental consent for the evaluation, or, if the State establishes a timeframe within which the evaluation must be conducted, within such timeframe; and

children.label: II
children.text: to determine the educational needs of such child.

children.label: I
children.text: a child enrolls in a school served by the local educational agency after the relevant timeframe in clause (i)(I) has begun and prior to a determination by the child’s previous local educational agency as to whether the child is a child with a disability (as defined in ), but only if the subsequent local educational agency is making sufficient progress to ensure a prompt completion of the evaluation, and the parent and subsequent local educational agency agree to a specific time when the evaluation will be completed; orsection 1401 of this title

children.label: II
children.text: the parent of a child repeatedly fails or refuses to produce the child for the evaluation.

children.heading: Consent for initial evaluation
children.label: I Consent for initial evaluation
children.text: The agency proposing to conduct an initial evaluation to determine if the child qualifies as a child with a disability as defined in shall obtain informed consent from the parent of such child before conducting the evaluation. Parental consent for evaluation shall not be construed as consent for placement for receipt of special education and related services.section 1401 of this title

children.heading: Consent for services
children.label: II Consent for services
children.text: An agency that is responsible for making a free appropriate public education available to a child with a disability under this subchapter shall seek to obtain informed consent from the parent of such child before providing special education and related services to the child.

children.heading: For initial evaluation
children.label: I For initial evaluation
children.text: If the parent of such child does not provide consent for an initial evaluation under clause (i)(I), or the parent fails to respond to a request to provide the consent, the local educational agency may pursue the initial evaluation of the child by utilizing the procedures described in , except to the extent inconsistent with State law relating to such parental consent.section 1415 of this title

children.heading: For services
children.label: II For services
children.text: If the parent of such child refuses to consent to services under clause (i)(II), the local educational agency shall not provide special education and related services to the child by utilizing the procedures described in .section 1415 of this title

children.label: aa
children.text: the local educational agency shall not be considered to be in violation of the requirement to make available a free appropriate public education to the child for the failure to provide such child with the special education and related services for which the local educational agency requests such consent; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the local educational agency shall not be required to convene an IEP meeting or develop an IEP under this section for the child for the special education and related services for which the local educational agency requests such consent.

children.heading: Consent for wards of the State
children.label: iii Consent for wards of the State

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: If the child is a ward of the State and is not residing with the child’s parent, the agency shall make reasonable efforts to obtain the informed consent from the parent (as defined in ) of the child for an initial evaluation to determine whether the child is a child with a disability.section 1401 of this title

children.label: aa
children.text: despite reasonable efforts to do so, the agency cannot discover the whereabouts of the parent of the child;

children.label: bb
children.text: the rights of the parents of the child have been terminated in accordance with State law; or

children.label: cc
children.text: the rights of the parent to make educational decisions have been subrogated by a judge in accordance with State law and consent for an initial evaluation has been given by an individual appointed by the judge to represent the child.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: E Rule of construction
children.text: The screening of a student by a teacher or specialist to determine appropriate instructional strategies for curriculum implementation shall not be considered to be an evaluation for eligibility for special education and related services.

children.label: i
children.text: if the local educational agency determines that the educational or related services needs, including improved academic achievement and functional performance, of the child warrant a reevaluation; or

children.label: ii
children.text: if the child’s parents or teacher requests a reevaluation.

children.label: i
children.text: not more frequently than once a year, unless the parent and the local educational agency agree otherwise; and

children.label: ii
children.text: at least once every 3 years, unless the parent and the local educational agency agree that a reevaluation is unnecessary.

children.heading: Notice
children.label: 1 Notice
children.text: The local educational agency shall provide notice to the parents of a child with a disability, in accordance with subsections (b)(3), (b)(4), and (c) of , that describes any evaluation procedures such agency proposes to conduct.section 1415 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: whether the child is a child with a disability; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the content of the child’s individualized education program, including information related to enabling the child to be involved in and progress in the general education curriculum, or, for preschool children, to participate in appropriate activities;

children.label: B
children.text: not use any single measure or assessment as the sole criterion for determining whether a child is a child with a disability or determining an appropriate educational program for the child; and

children.label: C
children.text: use technically sound instruments that may assess the relative contribution of cognitive and behavioral factors, in addition to physical or developmental factors.

children.label: i
children.text: are selected and administered so as not to be discriminatory on a racial or cultural basis;

children.label: ii
children.text: are provided and administered in the language and form most likely to yield accurate information on what the child knows and can do academically, developmentally, and functionally, unless it is not feasible to so provide or administer;

children.label: iii
children.text: are used for purposes for which the assessments or measures are valid and reliable;

children.label: iv
children.text: are administered by trained and knowledgeable personnel; and

children.label: v
children.text: are administered in accordance with any instructions provided by the producer of such assessments;

children.label: B
children.text: the child is assessed in all areas of suspected disability;

children.label: C
children.text: assessment tools and strategies that provide relevant information that directly assists persons in determining the educational needs of the child are provided; and

children.label: D
children.text: assessments of children with disabilities who transfer from 1 school district to another school district in the same academic year are coordinated with such children’s prior and subsequent schools, as necessary and as expeditiously as possible, to ensure prompt completion of full evaluations.

children.heading: Determination of eligibility and educational need
children.label: 4 Determination of eligibility and educational need
A
children.text: the determination of whether the child is a child with a disability as defined in and the educational needs of the child shall be made by a team of qualified professionals and the parent of the child in accordance with paragraph (5); andsection 1401(3) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: a copy of the evaluation report and the documentation of determination of eligibility shall be given to the parent.

children.label: A
children.text: lack of appropriate instruction in reading, including in the essential components of reading instruction (as defined in , as such section was in effect on the day before );section 6368(3) of this titleDecember 10, 2015

children.label: B
children.text: lack of instruction in math; or

children.label: C
children.text: limited English proficiency.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding , when determining whether a child has a specific learning disability as defined in , a local educational agency shall not be required to take into consideration whether a child has a severe discrepancy between achievement and intellectual ability in oral expression, listening comprehension, written expression, basic reading skill, reading comprehension, mathematical calculation, or mathematical reasoning.section 1406(b) of this titlesection 1401 of this title

children.heading: Additional authority
children.label: B Additional authority
children.text: In determining whether a child has a specific learning disability, a local educational agency may use a process that determines if the child responds to scientific, research-based intervention as a part of the evaluation procedures described in paragraphs (2) and (3).

children.heading: Additional requirements for evaluation and reevaluations
children.label: c Additional requirements for evaluation and reevaluations
i
children.text: evaluations and information provided by the parents of the child;

children.label: ii
children.text: current classroom-based, local, or State assessments, and classroom-based observations; and

children.label: iii
children.text: observations by teachers and related services providers; and

children.label: i
children.text: whether the child is a child with a disability as defined in , and the educational needs of the child, or, in case of a reevaluation of a child, whether the child continues to have such a disability and such educational needs;section 1401(3) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the present levels of academic achievement and related developmental needs of the child;

children.label: iii
children.text: whether the child needs special education and related services, or in the case of a reevaluation of a child, whether the child continues to need special education and related services; and

children.label: iv
children.text: whether any additions or modifications to the special education and related services are needed to enable the child to meet the measurable annual goals set out in the individualized education program of the child and to participate, as appropriate, in the general education curriculum.

children.heading: Source of data
children.label: 2 Source of data
children.text: The local educational agency shall administer such assessments and other evaluation measures as may be needed to produce the data identified by the IEP Team under paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: Parental consent
children.label: 3 Parental consent
children.text: Each local educational agency shall obtain informed parental consent, in accordance with subsection (a)(1)(D), prior to conducting any reevaluation of a child with a disability, except that such informed parental consent need not be obtained if the local educational agency can demonstrate that it had taken reasonable measures to obtain such consent and the child’s parent has failed to respond.

children.heading: Requirements if additional data are not needed
children.label: 4 Requirements if additional data are not needed
i
children.text: that determination and the reasons for the determination; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the right of such parents to request an assessment to determine whether the child continues to be a child with a disability and to determine the child’s educational needs; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall not be required to conduct such an assessment unless requested to by the child’s parents.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), a local educational agency shall evaluate a child with a disability in accordance with this section before determining that the child is no longer a child with a disability.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The evaluation described in subparagraph (A) shall not be required before the termination of a child’s eligibility under this subchapter due to graduation from secondary school with a regular diploma, or due to exceeding the age eligibility for a free appropriate public education under State law.

children.heading: Summary of performance
children.label: ii Summary of performance
children.text: For a child whose eligibility under this subchapter terminates under circumstances described in clause (i), a local educational agency shall provide the child with a summary of the child’s academic achievement and functional performance, which shall include recommendations on how to assist the child in meeting the child’s postsecondary goals.

children.label: aa
children.text: how the child’s disability affects the child’s involvement and progress in the general education curriculum;

children.label: bb
children.text: for preschool children, as appropriate, how the disability affects the child’s participation in appropriate activities; and

children.label: cc
children.text: for children with disabilities who take alternate assessments aligned to alternate achievement standards, a description of benchmarks or short-term objectives;

children.label: aa
children.text: meet the child’s needs that result from the child’s disability to enable the child to be involved in and make progress in the general education curriculum; and

children.label: bb
children.text: meet each of the child’s other educational needs that result from the child’s disability;

children.label: III
children.text: a description of how the child’s progress toward meeting the annual goals described in subclause (II) will be measured and when periodic reports on the progress the child is making toward meeting the annual goals (such as through the use of quarterly or other periodic reports, concurrent with the issuance of report cards) will be provided;

children.label: aa
children.text: to advance appropriately toward attaining the annual goals;

children.label: bb
children.text: to be involved in and make progress in the general education curriculum in accordance with subclause (I) and to participate in extracurricular and other nonacademic activities; and

children.label: cc
children.text: to be educated and participate with other children with disabilities and nondisabled children in the activities described in this subparagraph;

children.label: V
children.text: an explanation of the extent, if any, to which the child will not participate with nondisabled children in the regular class and in the activities described in subclause (IV)(cc);

children.label: aa
children.text: a statement of any individual appropriate accommodations that are necessary to measure the academic achievement and functional performance of the child on State and districtwide assessments consistent with ; andsection 1412(a)(16)(A) of this title

children.label: AA
children.text: the child cannot participate in the regular assessment; and

children.label: BB
children.text: the particular alternate assessment selected is appropriate for the child;

children.label: VII
children.text: the projected date for the beginning of the services and modifications described in subclause (IV), and the anticipated frequency, location, and duration of those services and modifications; and

children.label: aa
children.text: appropriate measurable postsecondary goals based upon age appropriate transition assessments related to training, education, employment, and, where appropriate, independent living skills;

children.label: bb
children.text: the transition services (including courses of study) needed to assist the child in reaching those goals; and

children.label: cc
children.text: beginning not later than 1 year before the child reaches the age of majority under State law, a statement that the child has been informed of the child’s rights under this chapter, if any, that will transfer to the child on reaching the age of majority under .section 1415(m) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: that additional information be included in a child’s IEP beyond what is explicitly required in this section; and

children.label: II
children.text: the IEP Team to include information under 1 component of a child’s IEP that is already contained under another component of such IEP.

children.label: i
children.text: the parents of a child with a disability;

children.label: ii
children.text: not less than 1 regular education teacher of such child (if the child is, or may be, participating in the regular education environment);

children.label: iii
children.text: not less than 1 special education teacher, or where appropriate, not less than 1 special education provider of such child;

children.label: I
children.text: is qualified to provide, or supervise the provision of, specially designed instruction to meet the unique needs of children with disabilities;

children.label: II
children.text: is knowledgeable about the general education curriculum; and

children.label: III
children.text: is knowledgeable about the availability of resources of the local educational agency;

children.label: v
children.text: an individual who can interpret the instructional implications of evaluation results, who may be a member of the team described in clauses (ii) through (vi);

children.label: vi
children.text: at the discretion of the parent or the agency, other individuals who have knowledge or special expertise regarding the child, including related services personnel as appropriate; and

children.label: vii
children.text: whenever appropriate, the child with a disability.

children.heading: Attendance not necessary
children.label: i Attendance not necessary
children.text: A member of the IEP Team shall not be required to attend an IEP meeting, in whole or in part, if the parent of a child with a disability and the local educational agency agree that the attendance of such member is not necessary because the member’s area of the curriculum or related services is not being modified or discussed in the meeting.

children.label: I
children.text: the parent and the local educational agency consent to the excusal; and

children.label: II
children.text: the member submits, in writing to the parent and the IEP Team, input into the development of the IEP prior to the meeting.

children.heading: Written agreement and consent required
children.label: iii Written agreement and consent required
children.text: A parent’s agreement under clause (i) and consent under clause (ii) shall be in writing.

children.heading: IEP Team transition
children.label: D IEP Team transition
children.text: In the case of a child who was previously served under subchapter III, an invitation to the initial IEP meeting shall, at the request of the parent, be sent to the subchapter III service coordinator or other representatives of the subchapter III system to assist with the smooth transition of services.

children.heading: Requirement that program be in effect
children.label: 2 Requirement that program be in effect

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: At the beginning of each school year, each local educational agency, State educational agency, or other State agency, as the case may be, shall have in effect, for each child with a disability in the agency’s jurisdiction, an individualized education program, as defined in paragraph (1)(A).

children.heading: Program for child aged 3 through 5
children.label: B Program for child aged 3 through 5
i
children.text: consistent with State policy; and

children.label: ii
children.text: agreed to by the agency and the child’s parents.

children.heading: Program for children who transfer school districts
children.label: C Program for children who transfer school districts

children.heading: Transfer within the same State
children.label: I Transfer within the same State
children.text: In the case of a child with a disability who transfers school districts within the same academic year, who enrolls in a new school, and who had an IEP that was in effect in the same State, the local educational agency shall provide such child with a free appropriate public education, including services comparable to those described in the previously held IEP, in consultation with the parents until such time as the local educational agency adopts the previously held IEP or develops, adopts, and implements a new IEP that is consistent with Federal and State law.

children.heading: Transfer outside State
children.label: II Transfer outside State
children.text: In the case of a child with a disability who transfers school districts within the same academic year, who enrolls in a new school, and who had an IEP that was in effect in another State, the local educational agency shall provide such child with a free appropriate public education, including services comparable to those described in the previously held IEP, in consultation with the parents until such time as the local educational agency conducts an evaluation pursuant to subsection (a)(1), if determined to be necessary by such agency, and develops a new IEP, if appropriate, that is consistent with Federal and State law.

children.label: I
children.text: the new school in which the child enrolls shall take reasonable steps to promptly obtain the child’s records, including the IEP and supporting documents and any other records relating to the provision of special education or related services to the child, from the previous school in which the child was enrolled, pursuant to section 99.31(a)(2) of title 34, Code of Federal Regulations; and

children.label: II
children.text: the previous school in which the child was enrolled shall take reasonable steps to promptly respond to such request from the new school.

children.label: i
children.text: the strengths of the child;

children.label: ii
children.text: the concerns of the parents for enhancing the education of their child;

children.label: iii
children.text: the results of the initial evaluation or most recent evaluation of the child; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the academic, developmental, and functional needs of the child.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a child whose behavior impedes the child’s learning or that of others, consider the use of positive behavioral interventions and supports, and other strategies, to address that behavior;

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a child with limited English proficiency, consider the language needs of the child as such needs relate to the child’s IEP;

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a child who is blind or visually impaired, provide for instruction in Braille and the use of Braille unless the IEP Team determines, after an evaluation of the child’s reading and writing skills, needs, and appropriate reading and writing media (including an evaluation of the child’s future needs for instruction in Braille or the use of Braille), that instruction in Braille or the use of Braille is not appropriate for the child;

children.label: iv
children.text: consider the communication needs of the child, and in the case of a child who is deaf or hard of hearing, consider the child’s language and communication needs, opportunities for direct communications with peers and professional personnel in the child’s language and communication mode, academic level, and full range of needs, including opportunities for direct instruction in the child’s language and communication mode; and

children.label: v
children.text: consider whether the child needs assistive technology devices and services.

children.heading: Requirement with respect to regular education teacher
children.label: C Requirement with respect to regular education teacher
children.text: A regular education teacher of the child, as a member of the IEP Team, shall, to the extent appropriate, participate in the development of the IEP of the child, including the determination of appropriate positive behavioral interventions and supports, and other strategies, and the determination of supplementary aids and services, program modifications, and support for school personnel consistent with paragraph (1)(A)(i)(IV).

children.heading: Agreement
children.label: D Agreement
children.text: In making changes to a child’s IEP after the annual IEP meeting for a school year, the parent of a child with a disability and the local educational agency may agree not to convene an IEP meeting for the purposes of making such changes, and instead may develop a written document to amend or modify the child’s current IEP.

children.heading: Consolidation of IEP Team meetings
children.label: E Consolidation of IEP Team meetings
children.text: To the extent possible, the local educational agency shall encourage the consolidation of reevaluation meetings for the child and other IEP Team meetings for the child.

children.heading: Amendments
children.label: F Amendments
children.text: Changes to the IEP may be made either by the entire IEP Team or, as provided in subparagraph (D), by amending the IEP rather than by redrafting the entire IEP. Upon request, a parent shall be provided with a revised copy of the IEP with the amendments incorporated.

children.label: i
children.text: reviews the child’s IEP periodically, but not less frequently than annually, to determine whether the annual goals for the child are being achieved; and

children.label: I
children.text: any lack of expected progress toward the annual goals and in the general education curriculum, where appropriate;

children.label: II
children.text: the results of any reevaluation conducted under this section;

children.label: III
children.text: information about the child provided to, or by, the parents, as described in subsection (c)(1)(B);

children.label: IV
children.text: the child’s anticipated needs; or

children.label: V
children.text: other matters.

children.heading: Requirement with respect to regular education teacher
children.label: B Requirement with respect to regular education teacher
children.text: A regular education teacher of the child, as a member of the IEP Team, shall, consistent with paragraph (1)(C), participate in the review and revision of the IEP of the child.

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: i Purpose
children.text: The purpose of this paragraph is to provide an opportunity for States to allow parents and local educational agencies the opportunity for long-term planning by offering the option of developing a comprehensive multi-year IEP, not to exceed 3 years, that is designed to coincide with the natural transition points for the child.

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: ii Authorization
children.text: In order to carry out the purpose of this paragraph, the Secretary is authorized to approve not more than 15 proposals from States to carry out the activity described in clause (i).

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: A State desiring to participate in the program under this paragraph shall submit a proposal to the Secretary at such time and in such manner as the Secretary may reasonably require.

children.label: aa
children.text: assurances that the development of a multi-year IEP under this paragraph is optional for parents;

children.label: bb
children.text: assurances that the parent is required to provide informed consent before a comprehensive multi-year IEP is developed;

children.label: AA
children.text: measurable goals pursuant to paragraph (1)(A)(i)(II), coinciding with natural transition points for the child, that will enable the child to be involved in and make progress in the general education curriculum and that will meet the child’s other needs that result from the child’s disability; and

children.label: BB
children.text: measurable annual goals for determining progress toward meeting the goals described in subitem (AA); and

children.label: AA
children.text: a review by the IEP Team of the child’s multi-year IEP at each of the child’s natural transition points;

children.label: BB
children.text: in years other than a child’s natural transition points, an annual review of the child’s IEP to determine the child’s current levels of progress and whether the annual goals for the child are being achieved, and a requirement to amend the IEP, as appropriate, to enable the child to continue to meet the measurable goals set out in the IEP;

children.label: CC
children.text: if the IEP Team determines on the basis of a review that the child is not making sufficient progress toward the goals described in the multi-year IEP, a requirement that the local educational agency shall ensure that the IEP Team carries out a more thorough review of the IEP in accordance with paragraph (4) within 30 calendar days; and

children.label: DD
children.text: at the request of the parent, a requirement that the IEP Team shall conduct a review of the child’s multi-year IEP rather than or subsequent to an annual review.

children.label: I
children.text: the paperwork burden on teachers, principals, administrators, and related service providers; and

children.label: II
children.text: noninstructional time spent by teachers in complying with this subchapter;

children.label: ii
children.text: enhancing longer-term educational planning;

children.label: iii
children.text: improving positive outcomes for children with disabilities;

children.label: iv
children.text: promoting collaboration between IEP Team members; and

children.label: v
children.text: ensuring satisfaction of family members.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: C Definition
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “natural transition points” means those periods that are close in time to the transition of a child with a disability from preschool to elementary grades, from elementary grades to middle or junior high school grades, from middle or junior high school grades to secondary school grades, and from secondary school grades to post-secondary activities, but in no case a period longer than 3 years.

children.heading: Failure to meet transition objectives
children.label: 6 Failure to meet transition objectives
children.text: If a participating agency, other than the local educational agency, fails to provide the transition services described in the IEP in accordance with paragraph (1)(A)(i)(VIII), the local educational agency shall reconvene the IEP Team to identify alternative strategies to meet the transition objectives for the child set out in the IEP.

children.heading: Children with disabilities in adult prisons
children.label: 7 Children with disabilities in adult prisons
i
children.text: The requirements contained in and paragraph (1)(A)(i)(VI) (relating to participation of children with disabilities in general assessments).section 1412(a)(16) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: The requirements of items (aa) and (bb) of paragraph (1)(A)(i)(VIII) (relating to transition planning and transition services), do not apply with respect to such children whose eligibility under this subchapter will end, because of such children’s age, before such children will be released from prison.

children.heading: Additional requirement
children.label: B Additional requirement
children.text: If a child with a disability is convicted as an adult under State law and incarcerated in an adult prison, the child’s IEP Team may modify the child’s IEP or placement notwithstanding the requirements of sections 1412(a)(5)(A) of this title and paragraph (1)(A) if the State has demonstrated a bona fide security or compelling penological interest that cannot otherwise be accommodated.1So in original. Probably should be “section”.1

children.heading: Educational placements
children.label: e Educational placements
children.text: Each local educational agency or State educational agency shall ensure that the parents of each child with a disability are members of any group that makes decisions on the educational placement of their child.

children.heading: Alternative means of meeting participation
children.label: f Alternative means of meeting participation
children.text: When conducting IEP team meetings and placement meetings pursuant to this section, , and , and carrying out administrative matters under (such as scheduling, exchange of witness lists, and status conferences), the parent of a child with a disability and a local educational agency may agree to use alternative means of meeting participation, such as video conferences and conference calls.2section 1415(e) of this titlesection 1415(f)(1)(B) of this titlesection 1415 of this titleSo in original. Probably should be capitalized.2
